# Course data

Please include a short description of the data and instructions (e.g. code snippets) on how to download / access it.

In future where possible we can host these on zenodo




# Session 1 (introduction)

A 2.5 second NP2010 recording (50MB) that will preprocess and sort without crashing
and produces a few good units. Could be swapped out for the recording
used in other sections. Recording is available on github
[here](https://github.com/neuroinformatics-unit/course-ephys-osss/tree/main/book/data/pipeline-walkthrough) but
can be moved to zendoo.


# Session 2 (preprocessing)

The [IBL Benchmark dataset](https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.26348630) is a curated set of 11
Neuropixels recordings in varied brain regions available publicly via the
[Open Neurophysiology Environment (ONE)](https://int-brain-lab.github.io/ONE/) API.
Data can be streamed directly — no bulk download required.

The script below demonstrates how to:

1. Connect to the public IBL server and select a probe insertion by its `pid`
2. Stream a short snippet of raw AP-band voltage
3. Apply a Butterworth high-pass filter (300 Hz) to remove LFP/DC drift
4. Run the full IBL destriping pipeline (ADC shift correction + spatial k-filter)
5. Open all three stages side-by-side in the `viewephys` interactive viewer

```{.python include="scripts/stream_raw_ephys.py"}
```

*JZ* for the coding part the data from section 1 won't work as will need data that
is long enough to be motion corrected. Sometimes these recordings need to be
very long, in which case we might just work with the output but that would not be ideal.

# Session 3 (Sorting)


# Session 4 (Post-procesing)


# Session 5 (Pynapple, probe alignment)
